title: E I E I Annoyed Grunt

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Car crash during opening credits
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase of frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase in predictable context - high character integration but low originality

jokes.text: Oh, my. War is terrible.
jokes.setup: Sound effects suggest intense war movie violence
jokes.punchline: Comically mild reaction to extreme violence
jokes.explanation: Solid understatement but generic character voice

jokes.text: Buzz Cola, the taste you'll Kill for. Available in 'ze' lobby.
jokes.setup: War movie violence on screen
jokes.punchline: Corporate ad using 'kill for' after war imagery
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire with good timing and dark irony

jokes.text: Do they really think cheapening the memory of our veterans will sell soda? I have to go to 'ze' lobby!
jokes.setup: Character criticizes exploitative advertising
jokes.punchline: Immediately succumbs to the same advertising
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy joke with perfect setup-punchline structure

jokes.text: You're not supposed to go to the bathroom without washing your hands either. Touché.
jokes.setup: Worker refuses to put butter on Milk Duds
jokes.punchline: Customer points out another hygiene violation, worker concedes
jokes.explanation: Clever comeback but fairly standard logic joke

jokes.text: To the top, please. Swim, my pretties.
jokes.setup: Character treats Milk Duds like elevator buttons
jokes.punchline: Treats candy as living creatures in butter
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd visual gag but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: What? 'N'? What does 'N' stand for? No, no. It's a 'Z.' I am Zorro. 'Z' for Zorro.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to carve 'Z' for Zorro
jokes.punchline: Audience sees it as 'N' - Homer's poor swordsmanship
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's incompetence in character

jokes.text: I have come to return King Arthur to the throne. It's a history lesson come to life! No, it isn't. It's totally inaccurate.
jokes.setup: Homer claims historical accuracy
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately corrects the obvious anachronism
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Lisa dynamic with Homer's ignorance vs Lisa's knowledge

jokes.text: 'S.P.'? The Scarlet Pimpernel? We're just, uh, friends.
jokes.setup: Homer carves letters that spell something unintended
jokes.punchline: Awkward denial suggests romantic implication
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke but not particularly strong

jokes.text: You're pouring hot butter on my leg! Shh, shh, shh!
jokes.setup: Homer engrossed in movie with his butter contraption
jokes.punchline: Accidentally burning theater patron, shushing their complaint
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's obliviousness and movie theater etiquette violation.

jokes.text: Sir, I demand satisfaction. I challenge you to a duel. Do you accept or are you a coward? I... am a coward!
jokes.setup: Formal duel challenge with expected brave response
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately admits cowardice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - subverts expectation with honest cowardice

jokes.text: Whoo! You go, Zorro! I, King Arthur, declare Zorro the new king of England!
jokes.setup: Movie audience cheering
jokes.punchline: Absurd promotion of Spanish hero to English king
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical mixing continues the inaccuracy theme.

jokes.text: From the 'Z'to the 'O' to the double 'R'-'O' / He's the dude in a mask from the barrio / With his horse and his mask and his big ol'sword / He'll cut your butt from a '52 Ford
jokes.setup: Serious Zorro movie ends
jokes.punchline: Incongruous rap song about Zorro with modern references
jokes.explanation: Funny anachronistic mashup but lyrics are somewhat forced

jokes.text: Oh, move it, Q-tip! Ha!
jokes.setup: Homer leaves theater
jokes.punchline: Insults elderly man's white hair appearance
jokes.explanation: Basic insult humor, somewhat mean-spirited without much cleverness

jokes.text: Will you duel, or are you a coward? Would a coward do this? Bye!
jokes.setup: Homer challenges old man to duel
jokes.punchline: Runs away as proof he's not a coward
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic joke - Homer's twisted reasoning is perfectly in character

jokes.text: 'H' is for Homer! Thanks, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer carves letter in seat
jokes.punchline: Carves 'H' instead of 'Z', Marge recognizes the pattern
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier incompetence with letter carving

jokes.text: Mother, please! You're embarrassing me. No, I'm not. Seymour needs the toilet! His bladder's full- full of urine!
jokes.setup: Adult son asks mother not to embarrass him
jokes.punchline: Mother immediately does exactly that with TMI announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner family dynamic - Agnes's complete lack of boundaries

jokes.text: Heavyset? What's that supposed to mean? Take it easy. Take it easy. I'm just sayin'. You ain't no, uh, Tommy Tune.
jokes.setup: Moe gives directions referencing heavy guy
jokes.punchline: Escalates by comparing patron to notoriously thin dancer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Moe's tactless personality and obscure cultural reference

jokes.text: Hey, a duel? I, eh- Isn't that a little extreme? Here, here, have a free beer. Really? But you've never given anyone a free beer.
jokes.setup: Moe tries to de-escalate with free beer
jokes.punchline: Homer points out this is unprecedented for cheapskate Moe
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke about Moe's stinginess

jokes.text: And I owe it all to a little glove slap. [Musical sequence about glove slapping]
jokes.setup: Homer gets free beer from duel challenge
jokes.punchline: Elaborate musical number about the trivial act of glove slapping
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons musical parody - absurd celebration of minor action

jokes.text: Coming through. Challenge. Duel. Hi, Lenny. How dare you? Demand satisfaction.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers power of dueling
jokes.punchline: Casually challenges everyone while greeting Lenny normally
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy showing Homer's new obsession while maintaining friendships

jokes.text: Pistols at dawn? Oh, why did I have to slap a guy who says 'suh'?
jokes.setup: Southern colonel accepts duel challenge
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizes he picked the wrong person to challenge
jokes.explanation: Good recognition humor - Homer realizes his mistake through dialect clues

jokes.text: You'll need a will and a headstone. Aisle six. Aisle six? [Groans]
jokes.setup: Homer needs duel supplies
jokes.punchline: Store matter-of-factly sells death preparation items
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about commercialization of death

jokes.text: The National Weather Service says dawn is still scheduled for 6:20 tomorrow... and to please stop calling.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to know when dawn is
jokes.punchline: Government service annoyed by obvious/repeat questions
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist joke about bureaucracy meeting panic

jokes.text: Yeah, he's probably just a big blowhard like you. Sir, that is an insult! I challenge you-What?
jokes.setup: Bart casually insults Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer automatically starts dueling his own son
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Homer's dueling obsession to absurd family level

jokes.text: Oh, honey, I had the worst nightmare. This glove kept slapping people, and I was getting blamed.
jokes.setup: Homer describes his 'nightmare'
jokes.punchline: It's exactly what actually happened - he can't face reality
jokes.explanation: Good psychological humor about Homer's denial and self-deception

jokes.text: No, no, no, no. You'll get sap on your dueling blouse.
jokes.setup: Southern colonel offers to help with Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Marge treats formal dueling outfit like delicate clothing
jokes.explanation: Good domestic humor contrasting deadly situation with housework concerns

jokes.text: It looks like you were saved by a Christmas tree. And somebody wanted to get rid of it in April.
jokes.setup: Homer avoids duel through tree disposal
jokes.punchline: Bart points out the absurdly late Christmas tree disposal
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about procrastination and coincidence

jokes.text: You know, I've always wanted to tell you how much- [Phone recording] To continue talking, please deposit 10 cents. Aw, screw it.
jokes.setup: Homer has emotional goodbye moment with Flanders
jokes.punchline: Pay phone interrupts heartfelt moment, Homer gives up
jokes.explanation: Good anti-climax joke about technology interrupting human connection

jokes.text: That has people living in it. You can see them through the windows. Lousy showoffs.
jokes.setup: Family looking for shelter
jokes.punchline: Bart treats normal homeowners as showing off
jokes.explanation: Good poverty humor with Bart's twisted perspective on normal life

jokes.text: Could you build us a house, President Carter? We can't go back to ours. I'm sorry. These are for the truly needy. Yeah. Get your own habitat.
jokes.setup: Homer asks Carter to build them a house
jokes.punchline: Carter and volunteers dismiss middle-class family as undeserving
jokes.explanation: Good satire of charity selectivity and middle-class problems

jokes.text: Come on, Carter. Build us a house, you lazy bum! You have offended me, sir. I challenge you to- [Screams] Come back here! Why, you yellow-bellied-
jokes.setup: Homer insults former president
jokes.punchline: Carter also duels, Homer runs from elderly president
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing dueling culture spread even to presidents

jokes.text: 'Rural Route 9'? That's it! We can hide out at the farm where I grew up!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately needs hiding place
jokes.punchline: Road sign gives him idea for grandfather's farm
jokes.explanation: Functional plot advancement but not particularly funny

jokes.text: What a dump! Race you to the swimmin' hole! [Laughing] Come on, Ma! Bring the young-uns! There's no leeches! Ow, ow, ow! Lots of snakes though!
jokes.setup: Bart excited about country swimming
jokes.punchline: Immediate painful reality of dangerous rural water
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between rural fantasy and harsh reality

jokes.text: Holy moly! We can't stay here! It's full of raccoons! Just think of'em as cats with rabies.
jokes.setup: House is overrun with raccoons
jokes.punchline: Homer's characteristically poor attempt at positive spin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - making bad situation sound worse while trying to help

jokes.text: The Simpsons will be reborn as a bunch of gap-toothed bumpkins! I'll dig an outhouse! I'll weed the floor! I'll repress the rage I'm feeling! That's my girl.
jokes.setup: Family enthusiastically embraces farm life
jokes.punchline: Marge's response reveals underlying dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Marge's honesty and Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: It doesn't sound very tractor-y. Hang on. And there. [Clattering, chugging] Now we're talking!
jokes.setup: Tractor runs too smoothly
jokes.punchline: Homer makes it sound broken to seem more authentic
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy about fake authenticity

jokes.text: Go get Lassie! Lassie? I mean Lisa.
jokes.setup: Homer calls for help after accident
jokes.punchline: Confuses daughter with TV dog, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Decent confusion joke showing Homer's addled state

jokes.text: Oh, you poor little sheep! No one's sheared you for years. Soon you'll be a nice, cool- [Screams] Rat!
jokes.setup: Lisa compassionately shearing neglected sheep
jokes.punchline: Discovers it's actually a giant rat
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out misdirection gag with nice setup

jokes.text: Well, well. Look at the city slicker pulling up in his fancy German car. This car was made in Guatemala. Well, pardon us, Mr. Gucci Loafers. I bought these shoes from a hobo.
jokes.setup: Neighbor makes assumptions about Homer's wealth
jokes.punchline: Homer's corrections make him sound even more pathetic
jokes.explanation: Good escalating misunderstanding with Homer undermining himself

jokes.text: Well, I know your soil p.H. is up around 9.6, and you need it seven to eight max. Oh, that's just superstition!
jokes.setup: Neighbor gives technical agricultural advice
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses science as superstition
jokes.explanation: Good anti-intellectual humor perfectly fitting Homer's character

jokes.text: Not if you're plantin' gummy bears. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer confidently declares he'll grow something
jokes.punchline: Neighbor points out Homer is planting candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity moment with great visual gag setup

jokes.text: Maybe it needs more fertilizer. I'm only one man, Marge.
jokes.setup: Crops won't grow despite month of waiting
jokes.punchline: Homer implies he can't produce enough bodily fertilizer
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with efficient delivery

jokes.text: You couldn't grow stink on a monkey. Leave me alone, you big bully. Oh, I'm just razzin' ya. It's my way.
jokes.setup: Farmer insulting Homer's farming ability
jokes.punchline: Farmer's casual explanation of his cruelty
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing casual rural cruelty disguised as friendliness.

jokes.text: Oh, who am I kidding? I'm no farmer. Well, if it isn't Mr. Salad Fork. You couldn't grow stink on a monkey.
jokes.setup: Homer admits farming failure
jokes.punchline: Neighbor's colorful insult about Homer's inability
jokes.explanation: Good colorful rural insult with vivid imagery

jokes.text: More tumbleweed, Lisa? [Lisping] No, thanks. I'm still finishing my thistles.
jokes.setup: Family reduced to eating weeds
jokes.punchline: Lisa politely declines more inedible plants
jokes.explanation: Good poverty humor with Lisa's characteristic politeness

jokes.text: Wasn't skipping town in the first place a coward's way out? Oh, you're right! [Sobbing] I'm a coward!
jokes.setup: Homer says going back to Springfield would be cowardly
jokes.punchline: Bart points out logical inconsistency, Homer breaks down
jokes.explanation: Good logical contradiction with Homer's emotional breakdown

jokes.text: Plutonium? Gee, Homer, isn't that kind of risky? Yeah, I guess you're right. It's not.
jokes.setup: Lenny questions the safety of plutonium
jokes.punchline: Homer agrees then completely contradicts himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic failure with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: Hey, Lenny, sending some outgoing mail? You know it. I'll probably send some tomorrow. I hear that.
jokes.setup: Two characters at nuclear plant
jokes.punchline: Mundane conversation about mail while Homer steals plutonium
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist contrast between mundane chat and nuclear theft

jokes.text: Sometimes you have to break the rules to free your heart. You got that from a movie poster.
jokes.setup: Homer gives romantic justification for dangerous act
jokes.punchline: Marge identifies the cliched source
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about cliched movie dialogue

jokes.text: Well, when there's nothing left to believe in, believe in hope. Where'd you get that? From the producers of Waiting to Exhale.
jokes.setup: Homer tries another inspirational quote
jokes.punchline: Even more specific cliched source attribution
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but less funny than original

jokes.text: But didn't Grasshopperus kill Chad Everett? Only 'cause he tried to reason with him.
jokes.setup: Homer references B-movie logic about radiation
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation of movie death
jokes.explanation: Good B-movie parody with Homer's twisted logic

jokes.text: All right now, don't panic. It's possible the giant vegetables are invisible.
jokes.setup: No visible giant vegetables after radiation treatment
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate rationalization of complete failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer denial with absurd pseudo-scientific explanation

jokes.text: This farm's hopeless. Why didn't I listen to those inbred hicks? I guess I'll just go hang myself in the barn. Homer, no! Let him go, Mom.
jokes.setup: Homer admits complete failure and threatens suicide
jokes.punchline: Bart casually supports his father's death
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction humor with perfect Bart response

jokes.text: [Gags, coughs] Tastes like cigarette butts! Hmm. That's odd. The outside looks like a tomato, but the inside is brown.
jokes.setup: Homer tastes his successful crop
jokes.punchline: Disgusting taste reveals tobacco contamination
jokes.explanation: Good setup for central tomacco concept with visceral reaction

jokes.text: Gimme! I want more! I thought you said it tasted terrible. It does. But it's smooth and mild. And refreshingly addictive.
jokes.setup: Bart demands more despite saying it tastes bad
jokes.punchline: Admits it's addictive using tobacco advertising language
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of tobacco addiction and marketing euphemisms

jokes.text: Tomacco? That's pretty clever, Dad. I mean, for a product that's evil and deadly.
jokes.setup: Lisa acknowledges Homer's creativity
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts with moral judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment balancing praise with principles

jokes.text: Oh, Daddy! This tastes like Grandma! Holy Moses! It does taste like Grandma!
jokes.setup: Ralph makes innocent observation about taste
jokes.punchline: Wiggum confirms the disturbing comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Ralph moment with Wiggum's disturbing confirmation

jokes.text: Can I interest you in a mincemeat pie? They're very- Does it have tomacco in it? No, but I use only the- Pass!
jokes.setup: Vendor tries to sell traditional pie
jokes.punchline: Customer only wants addictive tomacco products
jokes.explanation: Good addiction humor showing complete market dominance

jokes.text: Kids are crazy about tobacco, but the politicians won't let us sell it to them. Those dirty, rotten- Tell me about it.
jokes.setup: Tobacco executive explaining their problem
jokes.punchline: Homer sympathizing with tobacco companies' inability to sell to children
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of tobacco industry with Homer's characteristically misplaced sympathy.

jokes.text: Dad, it's a tobacco company! They make billions off the suffering and deaths of others. She's right, Dad. They can afford a lot more.
jokes.setup: Lisa makes moral argument against tobacco company
jokes.punchline: Bart agrees but for purely financial reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling contrast - Lisa's morality vs Bart's greed

jokes.text: Did you see the way Emil's looking at Mindy? Hoo-hoo! I think something's going on there.
jokes.setup: Family discusses $150 million tobacco company offer
jokes.punchline: Homer is distracted by perceived office romance instead of life-changing money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - more interested in workplace gossip than massive financial opportunity. Shows his social focus over practical concerns.

jokes.text: The least we can accept is 150 billion! They'll be back. We control the world's supply of tomacco... and no one can take that away from-
jokes.setup: Homer confident in negotiating position
jokes.punchline: Cut off mid-sentence as animals attack the crops
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for hubris before fall with dramatic irony

jokes.text: Look! A flying saucer! Hmm? [Laughing] Hmm.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to distract animals destroying crops
jokes.punchline: Animals ignore obvious misdirection attempt
jokes.explanation: Decent desperation humor but fairly predictable

jokes.text: You're about to launch a terrible evil on the world! You've gotta destroy this plant! I know, honey. But what can I do as an individual?
jokes.setup: Lisa demands Homer destroy the dangerous plant
jokes.punchline: Homer uses environmental helplessness excuse
jokes.explanation: Good satire of environmental inaction and personal responsibility

jokes.text: Give them the plant, Homer! No. I'd rather die! [Screeches] Aah! Take it!
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically refuses to give up plant
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves when personally threatened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cowardice moment with excellent timing

jokes.text: So, Mindy, how did you want to pay me the 150 million? Cash'll be okay. Or stock!
jokes.setup: Homer expects payment for stolen plant
jokes.punchline: Tobacco executive has no intention of paying
jokes.explanation: Decent corporate greed joke but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Hey, props to Mindy on the grab! Boo-ya!
jokes.setup: Tobacco company steals Homer's plant
jokes.punchline: Executive celebrates theft with casual slang
jokes.explanation: Good corporate callousness with period-appropriate slang

jokes.text: Guys, we seem to be carrying a little extra weight. [Chuckles] Don't look at me. I'm on Sugar Busters.
jokes.setup: Plane struggling with extra weight from animals
jokes.punchline: Executive thinks pilot is calling him fat
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding humor with fad diet reference

jokes.text: [Sniffing] What is that intoxicatin' aroma? Could that be- I say, could that be mincemeat pie?
jokes.setup: Southern colonel arrives for duel
jokes.punchline: Distracted by smell of pie instead of fighting
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that resolves duel subplot peacefully

jokes.text: Hey! Are we duelin'or what? Oh, where are my manners? [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Homer impatiently asks about duel
jokes.punchline: Colonel remembers manners then shoots Homer
jokes.explanation: Good Southern gentleman parody with violent twist

jokes.text: Hey! Are we duelin' or what? Oh, where are my manners?
jokes.setup: Impatient about starting violent duel
jokes.punchline: Colonel prioritizing etiquette over violence
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Southern gentleman priorities.

jokes.text: You okay, Dad? I think so. The bone stopped the bullet. Dad, this might be one of those things you should go to the hospital for. After pie.
jokes.setup: Homer shot but conscious
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes pie over medical attention for gunshot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities with great deadpan family reaction

tmdb_overview: Fleeing from a duel, Homer takes his family to live on a farm. There he discovers that by mixing tomatoes, tobacco and radioactivity from the nuclear plant, he can create a dangerously delicious new substance.
